ref: Since Jacob was in love with Rachel, he answered, “If you will let me marry Rachel, I'll work seven years for you.”
text: Jakob ya'am da kpɛn' Rakɛl ka o ye, “M na tʋm yʋma ayɔpɔi tisif ka di'e fʋ biig Rakɛl onɛ an Lea pitʋ la.”
translated: Jacob said to Rachel when he saw her, “I will serve you seven years for your younger sister Leah.”

ref: Jacob was so frightened that he divided his people, sheep, cattle, and camels into two groups.
text: Dabiem nɛ ya'am nwa'ar da kɛ ka Jakob pʋdig nimbanɛ bɛɛ o san'an la zin'is ayi', nɛ kɔnbid nɛ niigi nɛ yʋgʋma la wʋsa.
translated: Because of the work that Jacob did, he was given a great reward, and he was also given a place of honor.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.

ref: Jacob said, “Now tell me your name.” “Don't you know who I am?” he asked. And he blessed Jacob.

translated: Then Jacob said, “I will not let you go unless you bless me!” And he said, “What is your name?” And he said to him, “My name is Jacob.”

ref: “How did you know you were naked?” God asked. “Did you eat any fruit from that tree in the middle of the garden?”
text: Ka Wina'am lɛbis o ye, “Anɔ'ɔn yɛlif ye fʋ anɛ biellɛ? Fʋ maal bɔ? Fʋ zan'asi m nɔɔr nɛ ka di tikanɛ wala ka m daa kpa'asidif ye mid ka fʋ dii li la nɛ bɛɛ?”
translated: Where is the money? Where is the food? Where is the medicine? I want to go and buy some and I want to eat and I want to be healthy.

ref: “My son,” Isaac answered, “I have made Jacob the ruler over you and your brothers, and all of you will be his servants. I have also promised him all the grain and grapes that he needs. There's nothing left that I can do for you.”
text: Ka Isaak lɛbis o ye, “M kɛnɛ ka o aan fʋ zugdaan ka kɛ ka o ba'abiis wʋsa aan o yammis, ka o na mɔr zʋʋd nɛ daam wʋsa. M biiga, bɔ ka m ye m lɛm maali tisi fɔ?”
translated: Isak said, "I don't know what you want to do to me. If you want to kill me, then kill me. I am ready to die. What do you want from me?"

ref: God said to Noah and his sons: I am giving you my blessing. Have a lot of children and grandchildren, so people will live everywhere on this earth.
text: Ka Wina'am niŋ Noa nɛ o biis la bareka ka yɛli ba ye, “Du'ami ka nɔbigi pɛ'ɛli teŋgbauŋ la.
translated: When Noa came to the place, he said to them, "I am going to the land of the spirits."

ref: This completes the list of Noah's descendants. After the flood their descendants became nations and spread all over the world.
text: Nɛ'ɛŋa pa'an Noa biis la dɔɔdi da dɔlis tuŋ-tuŋ si'em, ban da bɛɛ ba mɛŋ teŋin la. Ku'om da di dunia la nya'aŋ, nidib da nɔbigi pɛ'ɛl dunia la wʋsa.
translated: The world is full of things that are good and bad, so we should be careful about what we do. The world is a place of both joy and sorrow, so we should be prepared for both.
